sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper
Source code implements Algorithm 1 exactly: maintains m_t and v_t with updates m_t = β1·m_{t-1} + (1-β1)·g_t and v_t = β2·v_{t-1} + (1-β2)·g_t^2 (elementwise square).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Code applies bias-correction m̂_t = m_t/(1-β1^t) and v̂_t = v_t/(1-β2^t), and the parameter update θ_t = θ_{t-1} - α·m̂_t/(√v̂_t + ε) as in Algorithm 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Default hyperparameters α=0.001, β1=0.9, β2=0.999, ε=1e-8 are set in code as documented defaults for the Adam optimizer.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: AdaMax (Algorithm 2) is implemented with u_t = max(β2·u_{t-1}, |g_t|) and update θ_t = θ_{t-1} - (α/(1-β1^t))·m_t/u_t, with defaults α=0.002, β1=0.9, β2=0.999.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: m_0 and v_0 (and u_0 for AdaMax) are initialized to zero vectors, matching the paper's initialization for the bias-correction derivation.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity
MNIST is loaded and used as 784-dim image vectors for the logistic regression experiment (Section 6.1) with minibatch size 128.
IMDB movie review dataset (Maas et al., 2011) is preprocessed into 10,000-word bag-of-words feature vectors with 50% dropout noise applied during training (Section 6.1).
CIFAR-10 input images are whitened and dropout is applied to the input and fully connected layers, with minibatch size 128 (Section 6.3).
MNIST images are used for the multilayer neural network experiment (Section 6.2) with minibatch size 128.
Experiment execution and reproducibility
Logistic regression experiment is executed with αt = α/√t stepsize decay schedule comparing Adam vs SGD+Nesterov vs AdaGrad on MNIST, and vs RMSProp added for IMDB.

Multilayer neural network experiment trains a 2-hidden-layer fully-connected network with 1000 ReLU units per layer on MNIST, both with dropout and with deterministic cross-entropy + L2 weight decay variants.
CNN experiment runs the c64-c64-c128-1000 architecture (three stages of 5x5 conv + 3x3 max pool stride 2, then 1000-unit ReLU FC) on CIFAR-10 for ~45 epochs.

Bias-correction ablation (Section 6.4) trains a VAE (single hidden layer, 500 softplus units, 50-dim Gaussian latent) sweeping β1 ∈ [0, 0.9], β2 ∈ {0.99, 0.999, 0.9999}, log10(α) ∈ [-5,...,-1], with and without bias-correction.
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness
Training cost is reported as negative log likelihood / cross-entropy training loss vs iterations-over-entire-dataset, matching the y/x-axes of Figures 1–3.
For each compared optimizer, hyperparameters (learning rate, momentum) are searched over a dense grid and the best setting is reported, as specified in Section 6.
VAE bias-correction ablation reports loss as a function of log10(α) after both 10 epochs and 100 epochs (the two panels of Figure 4), separately for bias-corrected and non-bias-corrected variants.
Result match versus the paper's reported targets
On MNIST logistic regression, Adam's training NLL curve matches SGD+Nesterov closely and both beat AdaGrad, reproducing the ordering shown in Figure 1 (left).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On IMDB BoW logistic regression with dropout, Adam converges as fast as AdaGrad and both substantially outperform SGD+Nesterov, as in Figure 1 (right).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On MNIST MLP with dropout, Adam achieves lower training cost than AdaGrad, RMSProp, SGD+Nesterov, and AdaDelta, reproducing Figure 2(a).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: On CIFAR-10 CNN over 45 epochs, Adam and SGD+Nesterov converge to substantially lower training cost than AdaGrad, reproducing Figure 3 (right).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: VAE ablation reproduces Figure 4: removing bias correction causes instability/higher loss for β2 close to 1 (especially β2=0.9999) at small α, while bias-corrected Adam is stable across the sweep.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance
Repository contains runnable scripts (or notebooks) that reproduce each of Figures 1, 2, 3, and 4 end-to-end.
Plots of training cost vs iterations are saved as artifacts and reference the paper figure they reproduce (Fig 1 / 2 / 3 / 4).
